recommendations.title: The Scarecrow
recommendations.overview: Two farmhands compete for the love of the farmer's daughter.

recommendations.title: The Boat
recommendations.overview: A boat builder and his family attempt to set sail in his handmade boat, 'The Damfino'.

recommendations.title: The Electric House
recommendations.overview: Botany major Buster mistakenly graduates in electrical engineering and is hired to wire a new home.

recommendations.title: Cops
recommendations.overview: Buster Keaton gets involved in a series of misunderstandings involving a horse and cart. Eventually he infuriates every cop in the city when he accidentally interrupts a police parade.

recommendations.title: The Haunted House
recommendations.overview: A bank teller becomes involved with a hold-up, counterfeiters and a theatrical troupe posing as spooks in a haunted house.

recommendations.title: Neighbors
recommendations.overview: The Romeo and Juliet story played out in a tenement neighborhood with Buster and Virginia's families hating each other over the fence separating their buildings.

recommendations.title: The Play House
recommendations.overview: Upon waking from the dream of a theater peopled entirely by numerous Buster Keatons, a lowly stage hand causes havoc everywhere he works.

recommendations.title: One Week
recommendations.overview: Newlyweds receive a build-it-yourself house as a wedding gift—and the house can, supposedly, be built in "one week". A rejected suitor secretly re-numbers packing crates, and the husband struggles to assemble the house according to this new 'arrangem

recommendations.title: The High Sign
recommendations.overview: Buster is thrown off a train near an amusement park. There he gets a job in a shooting gallery run by the Blinking Buzzards mob. Ordered to kill a businessman, he winds up protecting the man and his daughter by outfitting their home with trick device

recommendations.title: Three Ages
recommendations.overview: The rituals of courtship, romantic rivalry, and love play out three times as a man vies with a villain for the girl. In the Stone Age, the rivalry is set off by dinosaurs, a turtle used as a ouija board, and a round of golf with stones. In ancient Ro
